Title: Integration with Shopify - Inventory Integration between Shopify and Method - Sales Orders with Shopify
Description:
Customer is currently using Shopify as their primary inventory management tool.

They do not have a full integration with QBooks inventory due to perceived limitations. 

They are wondering what the Zapier or API avenue could look like if they want to connect Shopify and Method directly with a focus on items.

In essence, when a item is added to a Work Order or invoice in Method, can we update Shopify inventory and verse versa.
Solution:
Shopify does not provide any information about items or item levels via zapier.

Therefore this needs to be a direct API integration.
Exploring and implementing any API is generally a large project.

A quick look at the API does look like this is possible:
https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-rest/2023-10/resources/inventorylevel

This wouldn't be a realtime sync but most likely a nightly or daily process.

Title: Add Credit Card Processing Fee to Invoices.
Description:
Currently, the ability to add a convenience fee for paying by credit card is not available in QuickBooks Online. As a workaround, you would need to create a service item for the credit card convenience fee and manually add it as a line item on your customer's invoice to indicate the fee.

Can this be done automatically if a customer choose CC payment on Method Portal?

If this is not possible then can we add something like how we have in our Donor Page App?
Solution:
Yes this can be done in the Customer Portal for when a Customer is paying off an Invoice. 

First we would need a table to hold all the possible Credit Cards that they accept. This table should hold fields like Name, Percentage Amount, Is Active. I would recommend being able to work with this table in either the Invoice Preferences or Payment Preferences.

Next we would need to have this table appear on Guest - View Invoice. We would show a dropdown where they can select which Credit Card they are going to use. On Apply, this will update the Invoice to show a new Invoice Line for the Credit Card charge. The Percentage Amount will work with the Invoice's Subtotal to come up with it's Rate. We would need to add screen logic to Show/Hide these payment options based off if a Credit Card charge has already been applied or if the Balance Remaining = 0.

If you want to apply this to do a Payment for your entire balance or for a bunch of selected Invoices off the Guest - Transaction List screen, this will balloon the time estimate.

Title: Email Integration App - Inbox Integration App - Method Email inbox

With Pipedrive’s Sales Inbox, you can manage your email account without leaving Pipedrive. You also get powerful tools like email tracking, merge fields, custom templates and signatures. Emails you’ve sent and received are also connected and displayed in deal and contact detail views, so all communication with your customers is neatly organized and easily accessible. Save yourself typing and re-typing by creating email templates or use Pipedrive’s pre-made templates. A true time-safer. The Sales Inbox is compatible with all major email providers, including Gmail, Outlook (Hotmail), Microsoft Exchange and Yahoo.

Title: Email Improvement - Logging Emails to Method CRM using Auto BCC - Smart Email BCC
Description:
This would replace the need of the gadget to 'log' activities and allow for users to determine what emails they want to log to the CRM by simily just BCCing their CRM emails. For eg.

To: Augustyna Dobosz (adobosz@gmail.com)
BCC: powerplastics@methodcustomer.com

The BCC would then find the 'To' contact in the CRM, and automatically log their email underneath their contact profile. 

BCC to CRM is a function within many CRM that gives sales reps the option to track sent emails to prospects and customers. By blind carbon copying a designated email address onto a rep’s emails, they’re able to log email activity under the CRM record associated to that contact. Sales reps can have visibility into the history of a contact and how they’ve interacted with them in the past.

You can automatically log your emails to Salesforce using the Auto BCC feature in Front to associate emails to any open opportunities, leads, and contacts.

From Pipedrive:

eg. Smart Email BCC allows you to manually add specific email conversations to a deal or contact, giving you the freedom to select individual emails to be sent to Pipedrive. To do that, simply add your name@pipedrivemail.com email address to the BCC field of the emails you wish to see in Pipedrive.
Solution: This cannot be customized at this time.

Title: Commissions App

Title: Lead Conversion - Lead Reports - Conversion to Customer - Lead to Customer Report
Description:
So I want to track who I have presented to aka My Leads and then track and get a report of who became a client and who didn't.

Lead to Customer here is defined by purchasing some service so an Invoice.
Solution:
Certainly can be customized, where we can probably 

1.) Take the 'Entity Created' date
2.) Retrieve the 'First Purchase' date - First Invoice
3.) Calculate the difference in days

Then based on this date field if the # of days is not empty we know this customer converted and can calculate the:

Total # of First Purchase Dates / Total of # of Entities creates on that Month

Title: Duplicate Entity - Improve Duplicate Detection - Improve Duplicate Entity Conflict
Description:
We have customers that work together in tandem with QuickBooks, in some cases, they are creating duplicate entities without even knowing. 

For a example, I've created a lead called Method Integration; however, my QuickBooks team has after the fact also created another customer or vendor with the same name Method Integration.

Because the information exists in both QuickBooks and the CRM as different records, there is no easy way to reconcile these duplicates. As a user I don't even know these duplicates exist and are problematic. 

So the CRM user is seeing the Customer Lead, who they can't convert into a customer because of a duplicate conflict. 

And the QuickBooks users is wondering why is there a duplicate. 

This is especially problematic when it comes to customization, and you try and update the 'Customer' table. Because the table will have both entries listed essentially blocking the table from any updates until the duplicate is resolved.

Archived entities further complicate these workflows as well.
Solution: We do have a data clean up catalog available to help resolve this problem. It's about 80-90% effective; however, for archived entities we do need to work with support to rename and bypass the duplicate entity issue.

Title: Whatsup Integration
Description:
On behalf of  Vikram Mehta.

The customer was intrigued by our new SMS function but was wondering if WhatsApp is a customization option for the actual delivery. 

MMS is generally smoother, has read only feedback and provides the customer the option for responses, 2-way communication and reduced cost. 

Currently Whatsup is not available via Zapier. 

Is this possible via API? 

Any Guidance for Whatsup requests in the future?
Solution:
As of my last knowledge sending messages via the WhatsApp Business API is not as straightforward as sending regular messages through the consumer version of WhatsApp. Here are some key points to consider:

WhatsApp Business Account:
To use the WhatsApp Business API, you need to have a WhatsApp Business Account. This is different from a regular WhatsApp account.

WhatsApp Business API Provider:
Businesses can't connect directly to the WhatsApp API. Instead, they need to work with an official WhatsApp Business API provider. These providers are third-party companies authorized by WhatsApp to offer access to the API.

Approval Process:
Before using the WhatsApp Business API, businesses need to go through an approval process with WhatsApp. This involves submitting an application to be reviewed by WhatsApp.

Compliance:
Businesses using the API need to comply with WhatsApp's policies and guidelines. This includes adhering to messaging rules, opt-in requirements, and other policies to prevent spam.

Message Templates:
Messages sent through the WhatsApp Business API often require the use of message templates. These templates need to be pre-approved by WhatsApp and can only be used for specific types of messages, such as transactional or customer service-related communications.

Costs:
Using the WhatsApp Business API is not free, and costs can vary based on the provider, the volume of messages, and other factors.

Technical Integration:
Integrating the WhatsApp Business API into your systems requires technical expertise. You need to set up webhooks, handle message templates, and manage the integration with your business processes.

Security and Data Privacy:
Businesses need to ensure the security and privacy of user data when using the API. Compliance with data protection regulations is crucial.

Use Cases:
WhatsApp Business API is typically designed for medium to large enterprises, and its use cases often involve customer communication, notifications, and support.

Title: Gross Profit / Gross Margin - Dynamic on Screen
Description: The customer wants to see the gross margin and profit dynamically on screen as they are creating an estimate or invoice. To make sure they're profitable when creating a transaction.
Solution:
Pretty Simple two new fields can be on the line or transaction basis:

Gross Profit = Amount − Cost
Gross Profit Margin=  ((Amount - Cost) / Amount * 100) 

Pretty easy to calculate these fields are the transaction is created.
​

Title: Full Advanced Inventory Support - Multiple Warehouse
Description:
Customer is a paving company and utlize the advanced inventory features of QB Enterprise. 

They would like to ability to sync and manage two warehouses.

They like the ability to use Work Orders or Sales Orders to reduce on hand quantity but would love it if they are able to see on hand for each warehouse and be able to pull inventory from a specific warehouse.
Solution:
At this time the ability to view Quantities by Warehouse is not available through the QuickBooks software development kit.

As a result nothing can be done.
That said this only applies to inventory that is shared between warehouses.
If all of Item A is in 'Warehouse' A then Quantity on Hand should be able to give the customer what they need.
We could customize a 'home' warehouse for each item.

Title: Batch Reassign Customers - Batch Assigned To Updates
Description:
As employees come and go, I need to make sure my customers are always assigned to an active Sales Rep or Assigned to. 

How can I move accounts from one rep to another?
Solution:
There are multiple flows for this:

1.) A simple export and import could help the customer achieve the desired result.

2.) Alternatively, we could customize this flow:

a.) Loop through all customers with the old 'Assigned To' or 'Sales Rep'
b.) Take note of both the old and new rep
c.) Create an activity to record the account was reassigned
d.) Update the customer

Note: The number of items wont add to the time, because we can set it up so they can add the images themselves

2 Workflows:

1. Add a text field
2. Track the URL where the image has been uploaded (custom website etc.)
3. Use HTML to render the image on screen / in a report

3. Add a button on the editable grid to show message - Show message retrieves image and displays it when button is clicked

Title: Portal  - Sub-Customer / Jobs in the Portal / One Email Multiple Accounts
Description:
I want to be able to see sub-customers / jobs in the Method portal.

The main parent should be able to see all of their invoices and the invoices associated with their jobs. 
They should be able to also pay for invoices.

subcustomers
parent customers
Solution:
Depending on the needs of this project this could expand to be a Medium sized project. Better to PAD when possible.

1.) Recommend removing any reference to 'Customer Balance' on the portal since this won't report correctly for all outstanding sub-customers. 
- I believe this exists on Customer Home & Recent Transactions
- I would remove the ability to 'pay multiple invoices' since this could create complexties later that are not needed.

2.) On Recent Trasactions Loop through all Customers where Parent Fullname RecordID = Portal Entity. RecordID 

3.) Using this list, I would loop through Customer and Build a List of Customer Fullnames and place it into a hidden field on screen.

4.) I would filter the transaction list for all the entities in this hidden object on screen. 

5.) Payment

When an invoice is opened and you click Pay Now the transaction recordID is passed to the Payment screen and should work as normal. 

Double check the correct entity is associated with the payment.

Title: API Integration - Shipstation
Description:
Customer is requesting assistance integrating Method with Shipstation for 

1) Calculating estimated shipping rates from Shipstation
- Sending information from Method to ShipStation
- Returning the estimate shipping costs to Method

2) Send orders from Method to Ship Station for fulfillment
- Keep a reference number in Method to link to ShipStation
Solution:
Possible - API WARNING - ALL INTEGRATIONS TAKE A TREMENDOUS AMOUNT OF TESTING. THE TIME ESTIMATE HERE COULD BE MUCH MORE OR MUCH LESS DEPENDING ON SCOPE. 

Possible workflow: 
1.) Button to send to ship station when the order is ready to be fullfilled
2.) We pass all the shipping details (Method to ShipStation)
3.) The costs are added there and the rest of the order is managed via shipstation
4.) The shipping cost can then be added to a shipping item in the CRM (and QuickBooks) once the order is ready to be shipped and used on the final invoice.

Title: QuickBooks Sync Improvement - Flat Discount Rate on Transactions - Fixed Discount Rate
Description:
My Customer would like to have a Flat Discount Rate as well as Percent Discount on All Transactions.

The ability to just type in $5.00 vs. having to type in 4.55% is a big deal.
Solution:
This can be customized, where we basically reverse engineer the percentage. 
However, there may be some rounding issues, as percentages only support 2 decimal places.

So something that should be $20.00 off might round down to $19.98 because the % discount field only supports 2 decimal places.

Title: Simple Price Rules / Price Levels (Method Only)
Description:
Custom Method Only Price Rules

1.) Fixed Percentage (% discount on everything)
2.) Quanity Based Discount (based on volume get 'x' price)
3.) Customer Specific Pricing (based on customer & item combination)

*** Note the customer will input pricing and price levels based on their need.
Solution:
Price Rules are complicated

This estimate assumes:
1.) They are not using Price Levels in QBDT 
2.) They are not using Price Rules in QBO (equivalent to Price Levels in QBDT) - We don't sync with these. 

===================

Price Rules can be very basic (Option 1)
1.) Create a custom table where the rules can be managed
2.) Create a screen where the rules can be edited
3.) Setup the format for the rules -- Fixed Percentage (5%, 10% or 20%)
4.) Every time an estimate, sales order, invoice is created.
5.) We would need to incorporate this into the item text change event. 

===================

Price Rules based on Quantity (Option 2)
1.) Create a sub-table to the item table 
2.) Setup the different levels and pricing for the item (0-5, 6-10, 10-20, 20+) 
3.) Every time an estimate, sales order, invoice is created we would need to evaluate the quatity and apply the correct sales price
4.) We would need to incorporate this into the item text change event & quantity changes

===================

Price Rules based on Item & Customer (Option 3)
1.) Create a sub-table to the customer table with a link to the item table 
2.) Setup the different items  and pricing for the item for the customer
3.) Every time an estimate, sales order, invoice is created we would need to evaluate the custom table see if the selected customer has specialized pricing use that sales price. Else, we'd default to the default item sales price. 
4.) We would need to incorporate this into the item text change event & quantity changes

Description:

Title: Integration with Shopify - Inventory Integration between Shopify and Method - Sales Orders with Shopify
Description:
Customer is currently using Shopify as their primary inventory management tool.

They do not have a full integration with QBooks inventory due to perceived limitations. 

They are wondering what the Zapier or API avenue could look like if they want to connect Shopify and Method directly with a focus on items.

In essence, when a item is added to a Work Order or invoice in Method, can we update Shopify inventory and verse versa.
Solution:
Shopify does not provide any information about items or item levels via zapier.

Therefore this needs to be a direct API integration.
Exploring and implementing any API is generally a large project.

A quick look at the API does look like this is possible:
https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-rest/2023-10/resources/inventorylevel

This wouldn't be a realtime sync but most likely a nightly or daily process.

Title: Contacts List Improvement - Hide Vendors & Hide Employees from the Contacts List
Description: My team doesn't need information on customer or vendors, it just makes the entire contacts list confusing for those using it. They just need to see their customers and leads. Is there a way to toggle off the things I don't need them to see.
Solution:
Small Customization

1.) We can update each of the view filters to include EntityType contains Customer
2.) Also remove any Vendor specific view filters from the list 

Pretty simple customization.

Title: Leave blank to Auto-calculate - Blank Transaction Number - Blank Estimate Number - Blank Invoice Number
Description:
The customer contacted us because their estimate numbers were not populating even though the screen says that if we leave the RefNumber field empty the number should auto calculate. 

Unfortunately this is not the case because the transaction could:

1.) Be wait for sync approval (and method numbering is turned off)
2.) Custom Numbering Might be on in QuickBooks
3.) The Transaction might be in conflict
Solution: Unfortunately, at this time, there is no easy way to customize a warning to the user explaining why their transaction is not having a number assigned.

Title: Simple Price Rules / Price Levels (Method Only)
Description:
Custom Method Only Price Rules

1.) Fixed Percentage (% discount on everything)
2.) Quanity Based Discount (based on volume get 'x' price)
3.) Customer Specific Pricing (based on customer & item combination)

*** Note the customer will input pricing and price levels based on their need.
Solution:
Price Rules are complicated

This estimate assumes:
1.) They are not using Price Levels in QBDT 
2.) They are not using Price Rules in QBO (equivalent to Price Levels in QBDT) - We don't sync with these. 

===================

Price Rules can be very basic (Option 1)
1.) Create a custom table where the rules can be managed
2.) Create a screen where the rules can be edited
3.) Setup the format for the rules -- Fixed Percentage (5%, 10% or 20%)
4.) Every time an estimate, sales order, invoice is created.
5.) We would need to incorporate this into the item text change event. 

===================

Price Rules based on Quantity (Option 2)
1.) Create a sub-table to the item table 
2.) Setup the different levels and pricing for the item (0-5, 6-10, 10-20, 20+) 
3.) Every time an estimate, sales order, invoice is created we would need to evaluate the quatity and apply the correct sales price
4.) We would need to incorporate this into the item text change event & quantity changes

===================

Price Rules based on Item & Customer (Option 3)
1.) Create a sub-table to the customer table with a link to the item table 
2.) Setup the different items  and pricing for the item for the customer
3.) Every time an estimate, sales order, invoice is created we would need to evaluate the custom table see if the selected customer has specialized pricing use that sales price. Else, we'd default to the default item sales price. 
4.) We would need to incorporate this into the item text change event & quantity changes

Title: Commissions App
Description: This is a full blown commissions app. Using this app you can calculate commissions for your sales reps based on various things that are calculated using the app including total sales, total margin, margin performance, etc.
Solution:
Solution Available in the Customization Catalog

Commissions are incredibly personal.
This catalog solution likely requires some accompanying customization or may not even be a fit.

Lite - Simple percentage on Invoice Subtotal (can be set by rep / global)

Advanced - Takes Credit Memos, Target setting, and Profit Margin into consideration

Title: QBO - The ability to Track Inventory by Location - Warehouse - QBO Inventory Management
Description: The customer would like to know if  it is possible to inventory by location. Use case: 'Ideally the tracking would be part of live operations so that we're accurately able to fulfill orders from the appropriate warehouse'
Solution:
This request can have a relatively large scope but it and also be relatively simple, it all depends on the needs of a customer.

Easy - For example, we could just link an item to a location, and keep track of where that item is always stored. So we know that for waterbottles, it's always located in Warehouse A.

Difficult solutions require more thoughts:
1.) How is inventory tracked today?
2.) How is QuickBooks inventory Managed?

Inventory management is the supervision of a company’s inventory, including the processes for producing, ordering, storing, and selling products in the market. This includes managing the warehousing and processing of raw materials, components, and finished products.

The scope of inventory management within our platform is quite large.

Title: Add Credit Card Processing Fee to Invoices.
Description:
Currently, the ability to add a convenience fee for paying by credit card is not available in QuickBooks Online. As a workaround, you would need to create a service item for the credit card convenience fee and manually add it as a line item on your customer's invoice to indicate the fee.

Can this be done automatically if a customer choose CC payment on Method Portal?

If this is not possible then can we add something like how we have in our Donor Page App?
Solution:
Yes this can be done in the Customer Portal for when a Customer is paying off an Invoice. 

First we would need a table to hold all the possible Credit Cards that they accept. This table should hold fields like Name, Percentage Amount, Is Active. I would recommend being able to work with this table in either the Invoice Preferences or Payment Preferences.

Next we would need to have this table appear on Guest - View Invoice. We would show a dropdown where they can select which Credit Card they are going to use. On Apply, this will update the Invoice to show a new Invoice Line for the Credit Card charge. The Percentage Amount will work with the Invoice's Subtotal to come up with it's Rate. We would need to add screen logic to Show/Hide these payment options based off if a Credit Card charge has already been applied or if the Balance Remaining = 0.

If you want to apply this to do a Payment for your entire balance or for a bunch of selected Invoices off the Guest - Transaction List screen, this will balloon the time estimate.

Title: API Integration - SOS integration
Description: Customer is looking to explore a direct API integration between Method and SOS inventory.
Solution:
Depending on the nature of your needs SOS Inventory Integration & the API Model allows for a number of opportunities to integrate both systems. 

SOS Inventory should have an established and active connection to QuickBooks Online to ensure all three systems are communicating and sharing common fields across the board.

1.) Item Inventory Tracking (by System or by Location)
2.) Sales Order support and fufillment

The above are two common requests we often recieve; however, generally this is implemented in a One-Way Format.

SOS Inventory > Method - On Items & Items by Location
Method to SOS Inventory - On Sales Order Transfer
The opposite could be true; however, with our current experience it's generally better to move from Method to SOS. 

However it depends on the workflow.

Updates after a transaction has been 'Syncd' also creates a number of complex challenges. Customer opting for this integration should expect a considerable amount of time dedicated to development and testing.

One should expect with all integrations, that there will be cases where delays, changes or missing information may occur as a result of workflows or API Changes.

Title: Full Advanced Inventory Support - Multiple Warehouse
Description:
Customer is a paving company and utlize the advanced inventory features of QB Enterprise. 

They would like to ability to sync and manage two warehouses.

They like the ability to use Work Orders or Sales Orders to reduce on hand quantity but would love it if they are able to see on hand for each warehouse and be able to pull inventory from a specific warehouse.

Title: Transaction Improvement - Adding Site, Bin, and Location to all transaction screens
Description:
The customer is a heavy user of Advanced Inventory, and while we can't support it fully, they would like Location Site and Bin to be visible on all major transactions.

Sales Orders
Invoices
Solution:
Customizing this is quite simple; as it's just about adding a line item to the screen. 

For an ideal experience, we could create a custom dropdown which aggregates all the sites, locations, and bins. Assuming they have manually entered a number in QuickBooks once, we can then create a better dropdown experience. 

Alternatively, we could potentially use customization to link items and locations or bins together. To create a semi-custom experience to help entering transactions. 

That said the most basic implementation, which is the ability to view site, bin, location is a pretty simple customization.

Title: System Email Improvement - System Email Visbility -  Auto unblock / ability for a user to unblock users who are not receiving system emails
Description:
Today, we have a number of users contact us on a daily basis about system emails not being delivered. 

These are not emails that they're sending to customers. These are emails we (Method) are sending to their users for the following reasons:

Invite user / Inviting a new user
Password Reset / Resetting a Password
Portal Login / Login to the Portal Code

Admins who receive these communications are basically helpless unless a member of our support team, logs into send grid, and unlocks the email. There are a number of reasons emails are blocked from being delivered but the user is hopeless in the exercise of attempting to unblock their team.

Ideally, I would love:
1.) Clear visibility to system emails that are departing from my account
2.) The ability to potentially action these emails or at least understand what their status is

So as an admin I can take the appropriate action to help my user.

Send grid, Email server
Solution: Cannot be customized - Support can however assist.

Title: Automate Scheduled Emails - Deliver Emails Based on  Scheduled
Description: Customer is wondering if we can post date email campaigns and automate the delivery base on a schedule.
Solution:
Yes, this is certainly possible.

How they are scheduled and the number of emails will certainly impact the complexity and time estimate.

For example, our proposals app is scheduled to send reminders automatically every 7 days and cap out at a maximum of 3 reminders.

Of course, this can always be tweaked managed differently. That said, Method can imitate email automation suites; however, the customer should evaluate if customization is as cost effective as using other solutions or Emailing Suites that help better manage different pathways, cadences, and monitor analytics to drive behaviour.

Title: Email Automation - Email Drip Capability / Scheduled Email - Sequenced Emails
Description:
Hey PS!

Can we provide an estimate on how long it'll take for us to customize an email drip feature in Method. 

My customer's workflow is as follows:

Select Email drip timeline - 1 day, 3 days, 7 days, 2 weeks, 1 month, 3 months, 6 months (Calendar option)
Select activity type - email, call reminder, appt reminder
Create new / Select template - email templates, call scripts 

Create a flow example
Email template 1 goes out 2 weeks from invoice date.
< complete / add step >
Email template 2 goes out 2 weeks after email 1 unless a new invoice was shipped. 
< complete / add step >
Email template 2 goes out 2 weeks after email 1 unless a new invoice was shipped. 
< complete / add step >
Phone call reminder setup repeated every 2 weeks until new invoice or toggled off.
Solution:
I would:
Create a table that would story the workflow for example:

Email Drip Category, Email Order, Email Frequency
- Invoices, 1,  14 days, Email Content
- Invoices, 2, 14 days, Email Content

I think to get this 100% right would take us 5 to 10 hours of customization.
If we needed to do more than 1 workflow - It would probably take an additional 2-3 hours after we build out the intitial skeleton.

Title: User login - User Login Report - User Usage - Last Login
Description: Customer would like to know if it is possible to add the ability to see users last login.
Solution:
This is possible today via customization; however, it would require users to go down a certain workflow. 

For example, everytime the user clicks on 'Contacts' we could create a log entry to say they loaded contacts.

We can keep track of the first time they opened the contacts screen that day and the last time they opened that screen. This flow could be wired up to review multiple screens; however, it's probably best to +1 to provide a platform wide solution. 

Customization is however possible.

Title: Ability to Assign Work Order to Multiple Techs / Assign Activity to Multiple Users
Description:
How would assigning work order to multiple techs work? 

Can we customize it so that, each teach can individual pull up the w.o on the field crew app and add their own notes/ time tracking. 

 These notes/time entries should update the singular W>O that they are all working on. 

Keywords:
assign-to, assign to, multiple assign-to, multiple assigned to, multiple assign to
Ability to select multiple assignee
multiple assignees
Solution:
Can be done; however,may take upwards of 8 to 10 hours depending on details. This would be split up into two separate phases. 

Phase 1: Create the ability to assign Work Orders to multiple Method Users / Field Techs. 

Phase 2: Customized the Field Crew and Time Tracking  apps to allow Field Techs to view those Work Orders and track time towards the Customer.

Details
- Notifications and how should they be handled
- Are all technicians working off the same work order or individual work orders
- What is the experience for the user.

Title: Complex Custom Pricing Rules by Quantity (without QuickBooks Price Levels or Prices Rules)
Description:
Customer is looking to add price levels in Method. 
They are aware that it will not sync to QuickBooks but the invoices will reflect the amount based on the price level we will add. 

Attached is the excel sheet he shared as an example.
Price Levels can have many renditions:

1.) Flat % based price level by customer type
2.) Quanity based discount based on item
3.) A combination of both where a Dealer purchases 100 items where we need to evaluate the better price of the two

Quantity Based Pricing Tiers
Solution:
This will likely take 10 hours to complete in order to setup a dynamic + scalable custom price level solution. From the attached spreadsheet it looks like there are about 8 prices (or more) that can be used for each item. We would need to discuss details about their process and the attached spreadsheet to provide a more accurate time estimate and scope of work.

I would assume this is only going to be used on the Estimates app for quoting purposes. If the functionality needs to included in other apps then this will increase the scope of work and time estimate, i.e. Sales Orders / Invoices / Sales Receipts.

Title: Dropbox / Google Drive / One Drive Document Integration
Description:
Customer is looking for an app that would contain all of their documents related to training, marketing,operations and etc. 

These documents are currently stored in Dropbox and Google Drive.
Solution:
This really depends on how they want to manage the documents and how many Apps the documents can be attached to. 

The cleanest way to do this is creating a Documents App. This would have a Document List and New/Edit Document screen. We would then make a Guest - Document List and Guest View Document so that the Customer can access these documents in the Customer Portal. I would say this would take the Small (3 to 4) hours.

The goal here would be to build a scalable solution, where the customer could essentially search a list of documents and link back to Google Drive / Dropbox.

Method is not a document storage solution; while we support document storage, we can certainly create a complimentary experience that will work for the customer.

Title: Ability to Insert Line Items or re-sort Line Items on a Transaction
Description:
User would like the ability to insert a line item on an existing estimate / invoice.

Its important for their line items to flow in a certain order but they often forget to add certain line items during the estimate process.
Solution:
Estimate is Small (3 -4 hours) per transaction.

This is a big ask, believe it or not. LineOrder is a special field that QuickBooks doesn't really give us access too and is a super important field. LineOrder is heavily relied on for other specials features such a Grouped Items.

If they are not using Grouped Items, then this can be a rather simple request.

We would add 2 new buttons on the EstimateLine grid. One for Up, one for Down. When clicked, this will work with the neighbour's data. We would hold all the values for each line in Action Results and update the respected Record with it's neighbours data. That way the actual Record doesn't move, just the values have swapped. 

If they are looking for a solution that works with their Grouped Items, this will greatly increase the estimated time to 10 - 30 hours. The LineOrder in Method will not be the same as what they see in QuickBooks. We would have to update their Guest - View screens, PDFs and more.

Title: Sales Order Reporting Screen
Description:
Customer is a heavy user of Sales order. 

They would like to have a similar graphical report for Sales orders as they do for Invoices and Estimates.
Solution:
Depending on the nature of the reports the customer would like for sales orders. This time estimate could be higher or lower.

In general we should expect each chart to be configured to take about 30 minutes. 
So a normal reporting screen would take about 3 hours to design. 

Designing the charts are quite simple; however, the advanced elements such as the filters are the top of the screen are quite time consuming. 

This is also a great feature request.

Title: API Integration - Avalara - Avatax
Description:
Customer today sells both instate and out of state.

Avalara is critical for us to make sure we are compliant with all tax requirements both inside and outside of the state.
Solution:

Title: Bill of Lading - Chain of Custody - Tracking Shipment Delivery
Description:
A Bill of Lading (BOL) is a legal document issued by a carrier to a shipper that serves as a receipt for the goods being shipped, a contract of carriage, and a title for the goods. It includes details like the type, quantity, and destination of the goods, and it must accompany the goods being transported. The BOL is crucial in the shipping process as it is used to ensure that the carrier delivers the goods to the correct location and to the correct party. It can also be used in disputes over shipping, as it serves as proof that the carrier received the goods as described.

A Packing Slip, on the other hand, is a document that details the contents of a shipment. It lists the items included in the shipment, but it doesn't serve as a legal contract or title for the goods. A packing slip is primarily used by the recipient to check that all items listed have been received and to verify the quantity and description of the products.

Key Differences:
Purpose:

Bill of Lading: Acts as a legal contract, a receipt, and a title for the goods.
Packing Slip: Lists the contents of the shipment for verification purposes.
Legal Status:

Bill of Lading: Legally binding document that outlines the terms of the transportation contract.
Packing Slip: Not legally binding; purely informational.
Use Case:

Bill of Lading: Used by the shipper, carrier, and consignee to manage the shipping process.
Packing Slip: Used by the consignee to ensure all items have been received.
Contents:

Bill of Lading: Includes detailed shipping information like the shipper, carrier, consignee, description of goods, and delivery instructions.
Packing Slip: Includes a list of items, quantities, and sometimes item prices or codes, but not detailed shipping or contract information.
In summary, while both documents are important in the shipping process, the Bill of Lading has a broader legal and logistical role, whereas the Packing Slip is focused on item verification.
Solution:
This can certainly be customized at a high level we need to track some additional information on the load and items:
eg. Weight and Dimensions


We'd also need to track additional information on the carrier and dates.

Title: Sales Order Portal - ECommerce, Price Catalog, Custom Order Portal
Description:
1) Our customer who are retail stores. We would like them to enter their orders on a B2B System.

It could be linked to our website or a stand alone B2B where they could enter their orders.

This is the flow:

-They enter their USER NAME and PASSWORD
-Once in, they see a picture of the products, the products code, their net price and the retail price (MSRP)
-Then they can enter the quantities of each units they want in their basket.
-Once done, they confirm their shipping address that will appear in the basket. They enter their PO number, comments if they have, etc. and then they submit their order.
-An email is sent automatically to them stating that the order confirmation will be sent to them within 24 hours.


2)For example, we have about 1,000 ship to locations and we have about 25 groups of customers.

Each group of customers have +-15 products in their price agreement, but have different net prices.

For example, attach is a buying group, there are about 70 ship to’s/customers into this group and they each pay their own invoices.

In Quickbook I have them linked together, but always invoice to individual accounts.
Solution:
First thing to keep in mind is that this will never look as nice as doing this with an actual ecommerce platform. 

1) Having them log in with username and password - I would recommend just using the portal flow for this -- No customization needed
2) Set up a screen with an editable grid that has all the items listed, have a button to view pictures of the items, and be able to change quantities 
3) Have a submit button which takes them to a separate page to enter PO number, ship address details, and displays the confirmed items
4) Insert invoice (based on the part 2 customization) 
5) Send notification email to customer (Involves creating a email template) 
----
Altogether the above is roughly 5- 10 hours I might even go higher because aesthetics might be a huge factor here

Part 2: Custom price groups
1) Set up a custom table to hold price group info
2) Display this info either on the edit entity or view contact page 
3) Modify transaction screens to check if there is a price group in place and then pull the price of that individual item 

Having done this one before it's roughly 2-4 hours of work (depending on which transactions need it)


-----

Technically an easy workflow:
1.) Make a duplicate of the Guest Sales Order Page
2.) Call it New Sales Order
3.) Create an item drodown with a quantity text box where the user can select from the grid (if the item has already been added) or select from the dropdown list of items.
4.) Create a 2 fields on the item table
4a.) Portal Safe Name: This allows the end user to name their items differently than QB
4b.) Is Item Visible on Portal: This allows the end user to control what items are visible on the portal. 
5.) Create a button to submit the order and notify the rep, if the rep doesn't exist notify the admin
6.) Create a filter on the sales order screen for 'Wait for sync approval'.
7.) Create a status field for the sales order options.

Title: Sales Tax QBO - Improve the Sales Tax Experience for QBO
Description:
Everytime a customer hit's save on a transaction in QBO

• Sales tax is finalized in QuickBooks after transactions sync from Method to QuickBooks.
• QuickBooks uses Automated sales tax to assign taxes based on customer's location.
• Tax information selected in Method will be updated once transaction is synced with QuickBooks.
• Tax rate may change when going back to an estimate in Method.
• Tax rate may also change the county at times.

This is a problem because customers will receive emails with different numbers and tax values. Everytime someone hits save on the transactions it's reverted.
Solution:
All workarounds, aren't perfect; however, we might be able to customize a solution to help prevent this. 

On save, ask the user if they want to re-fetch the tax values for the transaction from QuickBooks or save without preparing for accounting. 

We could also adjust the save to behave similar to the print button, where we just give the user the option to print without saving before printing.

Title: Method Payments - Instant Signup & Collection
Description:
Summary
Enable businesses to sign up for Method Payments and start collecting payments immediately—without complex setup, third-party integrations, or manual configurations.

Problem Statement
Currently, new customers need to go through multiple steps to enable Method Payments, including external verification, manual configuration, and in some cases, third-party integrations. This process creates friction, delays revenue collection, and can discourage adoption.

Proposed Solution
One-Step Signup & Activation: Allow customers to enable Method Payments directly within Method:CRM with minimal input—e.g., business details, bank info, and tax ID.
Instant Payment Collection: Once enabled, customers can immediately create invoices, accept payments via credit card or ACH, and reconcile transactions within Method:CRM.
No Third-Party Requirements: Eliminate the need for external payment processors or plugins—everything should work natively within Method Payments.
Smart Defaults & Automation: Pre-configured settings for payment terms, auto-reconciliation, and deposit tracking to reduce manual setup.

Compliance & Security Built-In: Ensure compliance with PCI-DSS standards while providing fraud protection and secure payment handling.

User Benefits
✅ Faster Revenue Collection – Businesses can start accepting payments immediately, reducing time-to-cash.
✅ Simplified Onboarding – No complex configurations or integrations needed.
✅ Increased Adoption – Lowering friction will encourage more customers to use Method Payments.
✅ Improved Customer Experience – Reduces frustration by streamlining the payment process.
Solution: Cannot be customized at this time; however, is up for consideration with the product team.

Title: API Integration - SOS integration
Description: Customer is looking to explore a direct API integration between Method and SOS inventory.
Solution:
Depending on the nature of your needs SOS Inventory Integration & the API Model allows for a number of opportunities to integrate both systems. 

SOS Inventory should have an established and active connection to QuickBooks Online to ensure all three systems are communicating and sharing common fields across the board.

1.) Item Inventory Tracking (by System or by Location)
2.) Sales Order support and fufillment

The above are two common requests we often recieve; however, generally this is implemented in a One-Way Format.

SOS Inventory > Method - On Items & Items by Location
Method to SOS Inventory - On Sales Order Transfer
The opposite could be true; however, with our current experience it's generally better to move from Method to SOS. 

However it depends on the workflow.

Updates after a transaction has been 'Syncd' also creates a number of complex challenges. Customer opting for this integration should expect a considerable amount of time dedicated to development and testing.

One should expect with all integrations, that there will be cases where delays, changes or missing information may occur as a result of workflows or API Changes.

Title: Gross Profit / Gross Margin - Dynamic on Screen
Description: The customer wants to see the gross margin and profit dynamically on screen as they are creating an estimate or invoice. To make sure they're profitable when creating a transaction.
Solution:
Pretty Simple two new fields can be on the line or transaction basis:

Gross Profit = Amount − Cost
Gross Profit Margin=  ((Amount - Cost) / Amount * 100) 

Pretty easy to calculate these fields are the transaction is created.
​

Title: Add Credit Card Processing Fee to Invoices.
Description:
Currently, the ability to add a convenience fee for paying by credit card is not available in QuickBooks Online. As a workaround, you would need to create a service item for the credit card convenience fee and manually add it as a line item on your customer's invoice to indicate the fee.

Solution:
Yes this can be done in the Customer Portal for when a Customer is paying off an Invoice. 

First we would need a table to hold all the possible Credit Cards that they accept. This table should hold fields like Name, Percentage Amount, Is Active. I would recommend being able to work with this table in either the Invoice Preferences or Payment Preferences.

Next we would need to have this table appear on Guest - View Invoice. We would show a dropdown where they can select which Credit Card they are going to use. On Apply, this will update the Invoice to show a new Invoice Line for the Credit Card charge. The Percentage Amount will work with the Invoice's Subtotal to come up with it's Rate. We would need to add screen logic to Show/Hide these payment options based off if a Credit Card charge has already been applied or if the Balance Remaining = 0.

If you want to apply this to do a Payment for your entire balance or for a bunch of selected Invoices off the Guest - Transaction List screen, this will balloon the time estimate.

Title: Integration with Shopify - Inventory Integration between Shopify and Method - Sales Orders with Shopify
Description:
Customer is currently using Shopify as their primary inventory management tool.

They do not have a full integration with QBooks inventory due to perceived limitations. 

They are wondering what the Zapier or API avenue could look like if they want to connect Shopify and Method directly with a focus on items.

In essence, when a item is added to a Work Order or invoice in Method, can we update Shopify inventory and verse versa.
Solution:
Shopify does not provide any information about items or item levels via zapier.

Therefore this needs to be a direct API integration.
Exploring and implementing any API is generally a large project.

A quick look at the API does look like this is possible:
https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-rest/2023-10/resources/inventorylevel

This wouldn't be a realtime sync but most likely a nightly or daily process.

Title: Outlook Calendar - Outlook Integration - Calendar Sync - Support for Outlook Calendar

Title: Email Improvement - Logging Emails to Method CRM using Auto BCC - Smart Email BCC
Description:
This would replace the need of the gadget to 'log' activities and allow for users to determine what emails they want to log to the CRM by simily just BCCing their CRM emails. For eg.

To: Augustyna Dobosz (adobosz@gmail.com)
BCC: powerplastics@methodcustomer.com

The BCC would then find the 'To' contact in the CRM, and automatically log their email underneath their contact profile. 

BCC to CRM is a function within many CRM that gives sales reps the option to track sent emails to prospects and customers. By blind carbon copying a designated email address onto a rep’s emails, they’re able to log email activity under the CRM record associated to that contact. Sales reps can have visibility into the history of a contact and how they’ve interacted with them in the past.

You can automatically log your emails to Salesforce using the Auto BCC feature in Front to associate emails to any open opportunities, leads, and contacts.

From Pipedrive:

eg. Smart Email BCC allows you to manually add specific email conversations to a deal or contact, giving you the freedom to select individual emails to be sent to Pipedrive. To do that, simply add your name@pipedrivemail.com email address to the BCC field of the emails you wish to see in Pipedrive.
Solution: This cannot be customized at this time.

Estimate email
Estimate Automate

Absolutely, customizable.

1.) We would first need to establish the rules for the automation, do we want this to occur every 'x' days or do we want it to occur once, it passes a threshold. Maybe both?

Title: Simple Price Rules / Price Levels (Method Only)
Description:
Custom Method Only Price Rules

1.) Fixed Percentage (% discount on everything)
2.) Quanity Based Discount (based on volume get 'x' price)
3.) Customer Specific Pricing (based on customer & item combination)

*** Note the customer will input pricing and price levels based on their need.
Solution:
Price Rules are complicated

This estimate assumes:
1.) They are not using Price Levels in QBDT 
2.) They are not using Price Rules in QBO (equivalent to Price Levels in QBDT) - We don't sync with these. 

===================

Price Rules can be very basic (Option 1)
1.) Create a custom table where the rules can be managed
2.) Create a screen where the rules can be edited
3.) Setup the format for the rules -- Fixed Percentage (5%, 10% or 20%)
4.) Every time an estimate, sales order, invoice is created.
5.) We would need to incorporate this into the item text change event. 

===================

Price Rules based on Quantity (Option 2)
1.) Create a sub-table to the item table 
2.) Setup the different levels and pricing for the item (0-5, 6-10, 10-20, 20+) 
3.) Every time an estimate, sales order, invoice is created we would need to evaluate the quatity and apply the correct sales price
4.) We would need to incorporate this into the item text change event & quantity changes

===================

Price Rules based on Item & Customer (Option 3)
1.) Create a sub-table to the customer table with a link to the item table 
2.) Setup the different items  and pricing for the item for the customer
3.) Every time an estimate, sales order, invoice is created we would need to evaluate the custom table see if the selected customer has specialized pricing use that sales price. Else, we'd default to the default item sales price. 
4.) We would need to incorporate this into the item text change event & quantity changes

Title: Whatsup Integration
Description:
On behalf of  Vikram Mehta.

The customer was intrigued by our new SMS function but was wondering if WhatsApp is a customization option for the actual delivery. 

MMS is generally smoother, has read only feedback and provides the customer the option for responses, 2-way communication and reduced cost. 

Currently Whatsup is not available via Zapier. 

Is this possible via API? 

Any Guidance for Whatsup requests in the future?
Solution:
As of my last knowledge sending messages via the WhatsApp Business API is not as straightforward as sending regular messages through the consumer version of WhatsApp. Here are some key points to consider:

WhatsApp Business Account:
To use the WhatsApp Business API, you need to have a WhatsApp Business Account. This is different from a regular WhatsApp account.

WhatsApp Business API Provider:
Businesses can't connect directly to the WhatsApp API. Instead, they need to work with an official WhatsApp Business API provider. These providers are third-party companies authorized by WhatsApp to offer access to the API.

Approval Process:
Before using the WhatsApp Business API, businesses need to go through an approval process with WhatsApp. This involves submitting an application to be reviewed by WhatsApp.

Compliance:
Businesses using the API need to comply with WhatsApp's policies and guidelines. This includes adhering to messaging rules, opt-in requirements, and other policies to prevent spam.

Message Templates:
Messages sent through the WhatsApp Business API often require the use of message templates. These templates need to be pre-approved by WhatsApp and can only be used for specific types of messages, such as transactional or customer service-related communications.

Costs:
Using the WhatsApp Business API is not free, and costs can vary based on the provider, the volume of messages, and other factors.

Technical Integration:
Integrating the WhatsApp Business API into your systems requires technical expertise. You need to set up webhooks, handle message templates, and manage the integration with your business processes.

Security and Data Privacy:
Businesses need to ensure the security and privacy of user data when using the API. Compliance with data protection regulations is crucial.

Use Cases:
WhatsApp Business API is typically designed for medium to large enterprises, and its use cases often involve customer communication, notifications, and support.
